included: agent wallet and signed exchange messages
deterministic batch matcher and replay data
TxLINE primary and independent witness adapters
ArenaExchange Solidity contract and ARC Testnet USDC custody
isolated ARC API, middleman, MCP, database and role-wallet deployment boundary
excluded: AIR OTC financial ledger and settlement authorities
end-user device security outside the AIR Arena application

trustBoundaries.failClosedBehavior: rollback atomically and do not acknowledge
reject or resume the same committed root; never replace partial state
halt, wait through grace period, then deterministically invalidate
reject any result not derived from the immutable rule

trustBoundaries.target: control plane and ARC contract roles
trustBoundaries.failClosedBehavior: deny incomplete quorum, missing evidence, or conflicting role membership

data: shared authentication, logging, RPC, and deployment infrastructure only
trustBoundaries.failClosedBehavior: reject cross-namespace financial identifiers and credentials
halt settlement on disagreement or uncertain finality

trustAssumptions.statement: ARC consensus, EVM execution, and the pinned ArenaExchange bytecode enforce accepted transactions correctly.
trustAssumptions.validation: pin chain ID, contract address and runtime bytecode; require committed confirmations and RPC agreement
trustAssumptions.failureBehavior: halt deposits, withdrawals, batch finalization, and resolution
trustAssumptions.mainnetGate: independent Solidity audit, bytecode attestation and RPC/reorg chaos evidence

trustAssumptions.statement: The single allowlisted ARC USDC ERC-20 remains transferable and acceptable collateral.
trustAssumptions.validation: token-address allowlist, six-decimal interface check, transfer-accounting checks and cap monitoring
trustAssumptions.failureBehavior: halt new deposits and markets without redirecting existing balances

trustAssumptions.statement: TxLINE and the independent witness do not fail or collude identically beyond the committed grace policy.
trustAssumptions.validation: source authentication, independence review, timestamp and raw-hash replay
trustAssumptions.failureBehavior: deterministically invalidate; never let an operator pick a winner
trustAssumptions.mainnetGate: late, corrected, divergent, stale, and unavailable source tests

trustAssumptions.statement: The capped beta sequencer can see plaintext orders and may censor or reorder before batch close.
trustAssumptions.validation: signed receipts, canonical post-close publication, deterministic replay and censorship metrics
trustAssumptions.failureBehavior: halt on receipt or replay inconsistency and publish incident evidence
trustAssumptions.mainnetGate: threshold encryption or independently reviewed equivalent for unrestricted public value

trustAssumptions.statement: Managed workload identities, KMS keys, hardware wallets, and multisig membership remain uncompromised.
trustAssumptions.validation: inventory, access logs, rotation drills, membership reviews and least privilege
trustAssumptions.failureBehavior: pause, revoke, rotate, reconcile, and require incident approval before resume

trustAssumptions.statement: The durable log and database preserve every acknowledged command through process and storage failures.
trustAssumptions.validation: crash-after-ack, restore, duplicate-delivery and failover tests
trustAssumptions.failureBehavior: stop acknowledgements or resume from last signed durable receipt
trustAssumptions.mainnetGate: RPO zero and RTO below 30 minutes proven

trustAssumptions.statement: Other AIR products and ARC AIR Arena may share operational platforms but not financial roles, keys, tables, ledgers, contracts, or public SDK contracts.
trustAssumptions.validation: namespace, database-role, deployment-secret and contract-authority tests
trustAssumptions.failureBehavior: reject cross-product references and block release

trustAssumptions.statement: No jurisdiction, licensing, sanctions, consumer-protection, privacy, or tax conclusion has been approved yet.
trustAssumptions.validation: named independent legal and compliance review for the exact launch scope
trustAssumptions.failureBehavior: public mainnet and real-value launch remain blocked
trustAssumptions.mainnetGate: all legal launch approvals recorded with evidence

controls.evidenceRequired: EIP-712 domain, chain, contract-wallet and forged-signature tests
nonce, expiry, duplicate and conflicting-message tests
strict schema and unknown-field rejection tests
per-wallet and global saturation tests
integer double-entry and reservation invariant tests
retry and duplicate mutation tests
append-only privileged and financial event evidence
crash-after-ack loses zero accepted orders
service and standalone replay roots match
authority matrix and disjoint production membership evidence
role, token, solvency, arithmetic, replay, resolution-evidence and payout tests
custody minus liabilities remains zero through chaos tests
independent source stale and divergence tests
immutable raw payload hashes and correction replay
no instruction accepts caller-selected payout outcome
KMS, hardware multisig and key inventory evidence
contract-role and fee-change timelock bypass tests
separate keys, namespaces, tables, ledgers, contracts and SDK contracts
RPC disagreement, lag and reorg chaos tests
wallet, market, treasury and global cap boundary tests
pauser cannot resume, resolve, transfer or upgrade
production API rejects private key material and uses unsigned transaction flow
fresh clone, SBOM, reproducible npm and Foundry builds, and zero unaccepted high or critical findings
independent legal, security, protocol and operations approvals

abuseCases.title: Forge or replay an agent order, cancel, or withdrawal
abuseCases.attacker: external client or compromised intermediary
abuseCases.attackPath: Submit a forged signature, reused nonce, wrong-chain or wrong-verifying-contract message, or conflicting client order ID.
abuseCases.impact: Unauthorized trade, cancellation, or withdrawal.
abuseCases.detection: Stable rejection codes and duplicate-nonce security events.
abuseCases.response: Reject before mutation, rate limit the wallet, and investigate key compromise.
verification: Day 8 EIP-712 domain, chain, contract, nonce, expiry, replay and ERC-1271 tests

abuseCases.title: Alter a market rule after users commit capital
abuseCases.attacker: market administrator or database attacker
abuseCases.attackPath: Change outcomes, cutoff, fee, cap, oracle mapping, or payout after validation.
abuseCases.impact: Unfair fills or redirected settlement economics.
abuseCases.detection: Canonical spec-hash mismatch and privileged-change alert.
abuseCases.response: Reject transition, halt affected market, preserve immutable evidence.
verification: Day 7 state transition matrix and immutable spec tests

abuseCases.title: Sequencer censors, reorders, withholds, or fabricates accepted orders
abuseCases.attacker: compromised or malicious sequencer
abuseCases.attackPath: Issue a receipt but omit the order, reorder after close, or inject an unreceived order.
abuseCases.impact: Unfair execution and loss of market integrity.
abuseCases.detection: Receipt-to-order-root reconciliation and public replay mismatch.
abuseCases.response: Halt batches, publish evidence, rotate authority, and resume from the durable cursor.

abuseCases.title: Apply non-conserving, duplicated, or root-inconsistent batch deltas
abuseCases.attacker: compromised settlement coordinator
abuseCases.attackPath: Change a delta, replay a chunk, skip a chunk, or replace a partially applied root.
abuseCases.impact: Insolvency, double credit, or unauthorized debit.
abuseCases.detection: Contract invariant revert or zero-atom reconciliation alert.
abuseCases.response: Reject or freeze partial state and resume only the original committed root.
verification: Day 17 conservation, chunk replay and partial-failure fuzz tests

abuseCases.title: Concurrent orders over-reserve or spend the same collateral or claims
abuseCases.attacker: agent sending concurrent or retried commands
abuseCases.attackPath: Race multiple requests before reservations become visible or retry after a timeout.
abuseCases.impact: Negative balances, overfill, or insolvency.
abuseCases.detection: Reservation constraint failures and reconciliation mismatch.
abuseCases.response: Rollback atomically, reject duplicates, and halt on invariant failure.

abuseCases.title: Resolver chooses a preferred winner or payout recipient
abuseCases.attacker: compromised resolver or colluding operator
abuseCases.attackPath: Submit a caller-selected winner, ignore divergence, or redirect payout.
abuseCases.impact: Theft of the entire affected market vault.
abuseCases.detection: Attestation/spec mismatch and independent source disagreement.
abuseCases.response: Reject, halt, invalidate after grace, revoke resolver authority.
verification: Day 18 test proving no caller-selected payout instruction

abuseCases.title: Use stale, malformed, corrected, replayed, or mismatched fixture data
abuseCases.attacker: provider compromise, MITM, or integration defect
abuseCases.attackPath: Replay a final payload, swap fixture identity, or hide a later correction.
abuseCases.impact: Incorrect settlement or indefinite lock.
abuseCases.detection: Source age, identity, signature, sequence, and raw-hash checks.
abuseCases.response: Halt and deterministically invalidate after the committed grace period.

abuseCases.title: Emergency authority becomes a seizure, resolution, or permanent-freeze key
abuseCases.attacker: compromised emergency pauser
abuseCases.attackPath: Attempt to transfer, resolve, upgrade, resume, or indefinitely hide the pause reason.
abuseCases.impact: Fund seizure or prolonged denial of service.
abuseCases.detection: Every pause emits an immutable reason and incident deadline.
abuseCases.response: Keep withdrawals of finalized unrelated balances available; recovery quorum controls resume.

abuseCases.title: Compromise default-admin roles or bypass release safeguards
abuseCases.attacker: compromised contract admin, CI identity, or release artifact
abuseCases.attackPath: Grant a financial role, change fees without the required timelock, or deploy a digest different from the approved artifact.
abuseCases.impact: Total custody compromise.
abuseCases.detection: Digest, signer, timelock, and deployment attestation mismatch.
abuseCases.response: Block deployment, revoke CI identity, rotate authority, execute reviewed rollback only.
verification: Day 29 key rotation and rollback drill

abuseCases.title: Protocol liquidity agent spends user funds or exceeds its market loss cap
abuseCases.attacker: compromised liquidity agent
abuseCases.attackPath: Submit orders beyond budget, borrow shared custody, or bypass reservation caps.
abuseCases.impact: Treasury loss or user insolvency.
abuseCases.detection: Per-market budget and custody reconciliation alerts.
abuseCases.response: Reject orders, stop quoting, isolate and rotate the maker wallet.
verification: Day 21 simulation proving loss cannot exceed funded budget

abuseCases.title: API, logs, database, or environment exposes a wallet or privileged private key
abuseCases.attacker: remote attacker, insider, or dependency compromise
abuseCases.attackPath: Read environment-backed ARC role keys, inject key material into an API field, or extract exported service credentials.
abuseCases.impact: Unauthorized signing and custody loss.
abuseCases.detection: Secret scanning, rejected-field telemetry, KMS access anomalies.
abuseCases.response: Pause, revoke and rotate affected keys, reconcile custody, notify incident owner.

abuseCases.title: Leak pre-close orders, wallet activity, secrets, or sensitive operational data
abuseCases.attacker: API caller, insider, or log reader
abuseCases.attackPath: Query another wallet, inspect verbose logs, or read plaintext orders before batch close.
abuseCases.impact: Front-running, privacy loss, or credential compromise.
abuseCases.detection: Authorization-denial and sensitive-field log scanning.
abuseCases.response: Revoke access, rotate secrets, preserve audit evidence, notify affected users if required.
verification: Day 27 logging and data exposure review

abuseCases.title: Exhaust order ingress, WebSocket, database, matcher, RPC, or compute capacity
abuseCases.attacker: botnet, Sybil wallets, provider outage, or pathological order set
abuseCases.attackPath: Flood endpoints, open connections, create oversized batches, or exploit retry amplification.
abuseCases.impact: Missed orders, delayed batches, or unavailable withdrawals.
abuseCases.detection: Queue depth, rejection rate, latency and provider health alerts.
abuseCases.response: Shed load by priority, preserve acknowledged orders, halt affected markets if integrity is uncertain.
verification: Day 26 provider and process chaos tests

abuseCases.title: Acknowledge an order or financial mutation that is lost after a crash
abuseCases.attacker: process failure, database failover, or operator error
abuseCases.attackPath: Crash after returning success but before durable commit or cursor replication.
abuseCases.impact: Repudiation, inconsistent balances, and missing execution.
abuseCases.detection: Signed receipt cannot be found in durable log or replay.
abuseCases.response: Stop acknowledgements, recover to the last durable cursor, reconcile before resume.

abuseCases.title: Compromised dependency, build step, image, or CI identity changes release behavior
abuseCases.attacker: malicious maintainer or compromised build infrastructure
abuseCases.attackPath: Publish vulnerable code, run install scripts, replace an artifact, or steal deployment credentials.
abuseCases.impact: Service or custody compromise.
abuseCases.detection: Audit, SBOM, digest, provenance, and fresh-clone gate mismatch.
abuseCases.response: Block release, revoke identity, rebuild from trusted source, rotate exposed credentials.
verification: Day 24 hermetic fresh-clone and SBOM gates

abuseCases.title: Act on stale RPC state, provider disagreement, expired transaction, or reorg
abuseCases.attacker: faulty or malicious RPC provider or chain instability
abuseCases.attackPath: Report false confirmation, stale balance, divergent block, or replaced transaction.
abuseCases.impact: Off-chain/on-chain drift or duplicate financial action.
abuseCases.detection: Provider comparison, confirmation depth, block-hash and reconciliation checks.
abuseCases.response: Halt affected settlement, wait for finality, retry idempotently or reconcile incident.
verification: Day 26 RPC disagreement and reorg chaos tests

abuseCases.title: Another AIR product identity, key, table, contract, or SDK path mutates ARC AIR Arena financial state
abuseCases.attacker: configuration error, shared-service compromise, or developer mistake
abuseCases.attackPath: Reuse a financial authority, database role, endpoint, table, or product SDK contract across products.
abuseCases.impact: Cross-product privilege escalation or accounting contamination.
abuseCases.detection: Cross-namespace API, database-role, secret and contract-address tests.
abuseCases.response: Reject request, block release, rotate leaked identity, reconcile both products independently.
verification: Day 14 dedicated AIR Arena SDK tests

abuseCases.title: Launch real-value public mainnet without legal, security, protocol, or operations approval
abuseCases.attacker: release operator, project insider, or compromised CI identity
abuseCases.attackPath: Set an environment flag, self-approve a gate, omit legal evidence, or deploy an unapproved digest.
abuseCases.impact: Unlawful or unsafe launch and uncontrolled user loss exposure.
abuseCases.detection: Release policy rejects missing independent approvals and mismatched artifact digest.
abuseCases.response: Fail closed, revoke deployment identity, remove public access, preserve incident evidence.

abuseCases.title: ARC resolver role can submit a caller-selected winning outcome without on-chain evidence binding
abuseCases.attacker: compromised resolver key or malicious resolver operator
abuseCases.attackPath: Call resolveMarket with an in-range winningOutcome that is not cryptographically bound to the committed MarketSpec and approved source evidence.
abuseCases.impact: Incorrect market resolution and theft of the affected market collateral.
abuseCases.detection: Resolution-evidence hash, MarketSpec, source quorum, transaction input and emitted outcome are reconciled before and after execution.
abuseCases.response: Keep public value blocked; require evidence-bound resolution authorization or on-chain derivation, rotate resolver roles, and invalidate safely on divergence.
verification: Day 20 stale, divergent and unavailable source tests

abuseCases.title: Exportable environment-backed ARC role keys create a single-service custody boundary
abuseCases.attacker: service, deployment, log, dependency, environment-secret, or operator compromise
abuseCases.attackPath: Read an exportable relayer, market-admin, matcher, or resolver private key from the service environment and use its ARC contract role.
abuseCases.impact: Unauthorized market creation, matching, resolution, gas spending, or operational impersonation.
abuseCases.detection: Role-wallet inventory, managed-signer attestation, access logs, key-use anomaly alerts and secret scanning.
abuseCases.response: Keep public value blocked; migrate role signing to managed non-exportable keys, revoke environment keys, rotate roles and reconcile every affected transaction.
